item.title: Morning Routine
item.description: I've been doing this every day, Mon-Friday for the last couple months. After I call her three "offices", I do the same with other Senate and Congress members. It takes me usually about 30 min to call all the different office lines that I know might not go directly to a machine. Later in the day I'll call the west coast offices. If someone answers, I say my line, don't give them any of my information, and I stay as respectful as the person answering allows me to be. MTG's Georgia offices are used to it by now, and at this point greet me by the name on their caller ID, and tell me to have a nice day when I'm done. Most reps are really respectful, however some are not. I really don't want to keep doing this because honestly, I feel like I'm being a child and not accomplishing anything. But then every morning I read something infuriating that they've said or done, and I'm back at it. FP Edit: I was almost hoping this would die in usersub, but thanks for the support! It still makes me nervous everytime I call, because I'm not trying to have my life and family affected by any sort of retaliation. I will post a few of the sites I use to find numbers, but all I do is google "'so-and-so's' office numbers" and go from there. I don't support cursing at, harassing, or name calling to the people answering the phones, who are more than likely unpaid interns. Some people will ask for a name and zip code, and I tell them "no thanks". I only call and talk with someone once a day, and don't repeat dial. I'll try a few times within the hour, and if no one picks up, I move on and try again the next day. https://greene.house.gov/contact/offices https://boebert.house.gov/contact/offices https://www.cruz.senate.gov/contact/office-locations https://www.hawley.senate.gov/contact-senator-hawley https://cqrcengage.com/dealer/app/person/310?0
item.dc:creator: MysterEF8

item.dc:creator: willCodeForUpvotes

item.title: Black man awaiting kidney transplant beaten by hospital security
item.description: The daughter of a late Black kidney patient wants justice against the security guards who attacked and wrongly detained him, according to the St. Louis Post-Dispatch. The shocking incident begins with Hughie Robinson heading back to the Barnes-Jewish Hospital in St. Louis after staff told him he left his wallet there. Just a few days before, the patient, who has stage four renal failure, got the good news: he was second in line for a possible kidney transplant. After retrieving his wallet, Robinson came back to the Kingshighway garage and couldn’t find his Buick. He asked the front desk and even a security guard for help as he continued looking for his vehicle. This reportedly caught the attention of other security guards, who started suspecting he was looking for cars to steal, reporters learned. That’s when the guards start approaching Robinson and forcefully grabbed his arm, which was tender from a procedure, according to a lawsuit filed last year against Barnes-Jewish. The then 52-year-old man allegedly asked them, ‘What did I do?’ before one of the guards tackled him, the filing claims. Then security allegedly started beating him up — including the guard tasked with helping Robinson search for his Buick. “Hughie cried out that the guards were hurting him. At least one of the guards responded, ‘Good.’ The guards then forced Hughie into a pair of handcuffs. All of this was on video,” the lawsuit alleges. The document says Robinson was taken to an interrogation room, where hospital security asked him what he was doing in the garage. The lawsuit also claims an officer used “his forearm to smash Hughie’s head into the wall” while another threatened the kidney patient. “If one of my officers, or WashU officers see you back on this property again, we’re going to do this whole thing again, cause you ain’t supposed to be here. You cool with that? … Don’t come back.” Robinson just finished a four-day stay at the hospital when the vicious assault happened, according to reporters, adding that he was drugged and “in a weakened state” when security allegedly beat him up. That following June, Robinson filed a lawsuit against the hospital for assault, battery, and false imprisonment, but he later died from his illness. Now his daughter, Chelsea, is seeking justice on her late father’s behalf. “It’s important for me to continue the case because my dad deserves to be at peace, and it’s what he would have wanted … his words exactly,” she told the Atlanta Black Star. “So, it’s only my duty to do right by his name.” She finally got to see the surveillance video after St. Louis Circuit Court Judge Joan Moriarity reversed a protective order that kept the footage from getting released. She says watching the video left her heartbroken. “He was just standing there, not doing anything,” the daughter told reporters. Source: https://newpittsburghcourier.com/2022/07/17/black-man-awaiting-kidney-transplant-beaten-by-hospital-security/
item.dc:creator: Igotfourbedrats

item.title: Libraries around the world thread
item.description:
Admont Abbey Library, Austria
Bibliothèque Nationale de France, Paris
British Library, London
George Peabody Library, Maryland
Tianjin Binhai Library, China
The Library of Trinity College Dublin, Ireland
Bibliotheca Alexandrina, Egypt
State Library, Melbourne
Stuttgart City Library, Germany
Baroque Library of Metten Abbey, Germany
National Library, Dublin
Biblioteca de Convento de Mafra, Portugal
Real Gabinete Português de Leitura, Rio de Janeiro, Brazil
Stiftsbibliothek Sankt Gallen, St Gallen, Switzerland
Bibliothèque Sainte-Geneviève, Paris, France
Rijksmuseum, Amsterdam
El Ateneo Grand Splendid, Buenos Aires, Argentina
Library of Parliament, Ottawa
Royal Library of San Lorenzo de El Escorial, Madrid, Spain
The Morgan Library, United States
Library of Congress, Washington DC, United States
John Rylands Library, Manchester England
The Vennesla Library, Norway
The Iowa State Law Library, Des Moines
Strahovská Knihovna, Prague, Czech Republic
Seattle Central Library, Seattle
Starfield Library in Seoul, South Korea
National Library of China in Beijing, China
State Library of New South Wales
New York Public Library, United States

item.title: Uvalde report: officers had training for this situation and knew what to do, but didn’t do anything.
item.description: Apparently the chief in charge #, Arredondo, and all officers knew what to do in the training. But the chief only checked one room and didn’t check the other rooms and told everyone it was a barricade situation with no kids. TL;DR: Almost everyone in this godforsaken department is an incompetent fucking moron. https://twitter.com/tracy_walder/status/1548787687804502016?s=21&t=8HFQqeFwUvZ_KWZkE9fD5Q
item.dc:creator: tfon12

item.title: BEST EVER broccoli cheddar soup (recipe)
item.description:
Love broccoli cheesey goodness? I've been making this for years... Simple, cheesy perfection.
Ingredients: 1/2 onion, chopped 1/2 stick (2oz/57g) butter 1/4 cup (34g) flour 1 cup (110g) carrots, shredded 3 cups (468g) broccoli, chopped 2 cups (16oz/475ml) broth (chicken or veggie) 2 cups (16oz/475ml) half + half* (yes, use it!) 1 tsp (5g) mustard powder 1/2 tsp (2.5g) paprika** 8oz (225g) shredded sharp cheddar cheese Salt and pepper to taste *I have learned that half and half is an American thing. International imgurians use 1 cup (250ml) heavy cream + 1 cup (250ml) whole milk. **I use hot paprika in this- I don't recommend using smoked as it will ruin the flavor. Omit if that's all you have.
Melt butter in soup pot. Add chopped onion Sautee 2-4 minutes
Add 1/4 cup flour to onion and butter. Sautee 1 minute.
It will be a bit clumpy- don't worry, it's fine.
Whisk in broth and half and half 1 cup at a time. A whisk helps break up any clumps of flour for the best texture. YOU MUST USE HALF AND HALF to get the texture of the soup right. This recipe isn't low calorie but it is DELICIOUS. Do it right and enjoy it. If you do (but I know you WON'T) sub for all milk, especially of lower fat varieties, your final consistency will be thinner.
After you have whisked in your broth and milk, add in the shredded carrots and chopped broccoli. Simmer on medium for 5 minutes and then turn heat to medium low.
This needs to simmer 20-30 minutes until the broccoli is tender. Tender, not mush. Stir frequently and adjust heat lower if it starts to bubble a lot. Don't roam far... just stand there and browse imgur like a responsible chef.
Add 1 tsp (5g) mustard powder + 1/2 tsp (2.5g) paprika. These help the cheese flavor really pop. You can make the recipe without these but it's better with them. Sprinkle in a bit of salt and pepper, too.
After 20 minutes, the broccoli has softened and the mixture has thickened nicely. Now its time to add your cheese!
I was extremely busy with work. I cheated and bought 8oz bags of pre-shredded sharp cheddar because it was easy and melts quickly and evenly and I was so tired. Please forgive this egregious error. For those with endless arm muscles and time, a block of Cabot extra sharp cheddar is sublime in this recipe.
Fold in the cheese... just fold it in! You know how! As soon as its incorporated, you can add salt and pepper to your taste preferences and voila- soup is ready!
Yummmmmmmm
I serve mine with a hearty rustic bread Bread Recipe: http://imgur.com/gallery/VPQZIta That's it! Only takes about 35 minutes from start to finish. I could eat this recipe every day and leftovers are fantastic.
Cat tax: all that cooking has tuckered Jerry out. He's napping his best! Jerry would like to politely remind imgurians to hit both the favorite and the updoot... many thanks, friends.... Stay weird, imgur! Imgurians love recipes! I have a few more to share: Dutch Baby Pancake: https://imgur.com/gallery/Dn65mTJ Best Chocolate Chip Cookies: https://imgur.com/gallery/ivP1O35 German Chocolate Cupcakes: https://imgur.com/gallery/T9G9kaD
item.dc:creator: EnigmaWearingHeels

item.title: Russia Makes Up Outlandish Story To Expalin Why They're Getting Their Asses Kicked In Ukraine
item.description: https://www.thedailybeast.com/russia-says-its-losing-because-ukraine-has-experimental-mutant-troops-created-in-secret-biolabss?ref=scroll This reads like sci-fi but we all know it's delusions of a madman with brain cancer. This isn't Putin's first claim of super soldiers either. This is from 2017: https://www.bbc.com/news/world-55905354
